column8: When the British travel in their own country for a holiday, what's their favourite destination? This topic could lead to a______________discussion, but one place that often wins awards is St Ives. (LENGTH)
column9: St Ives is a town in Cornwall, the most south-westerly country in England. It lies on the Celtic Sea the tip of the Cornish peninsula, and its white-sand beaches and year-round pleasant weather make it seem____________in comparison to the rest of Britain. (TROPICS)
column10: The town has a rich past, and some notable buildings date back centuries. A fisherman's tavern known as the Sloop Inn is believed to have been built in 1312 and has displayed the____________of local artists for many years. (PAINT)
column11: Although mostly regarded as a resort town now, St Ives was_____________a fishing port, one of the largest in the area. Billions of fish have been exported from the town over the years. (HISTORY)
column12: Dozens of artists make St Ives their home. They gain much____________from looking out to the open seas or at the lovely landscaped hills on the inland side. (INSPIRE)
column13: The work that the artists of St Ives do is so_____________that a Tate gallery has been founded in the town and documentaries have been made to showcase the artists' lives. (IMPRESS)
column14: Is our planet's population growth ever going to slow down? It's_______________to think about,but, by 2083, the number of people on the planet is expected to reach 10 billion. (TERRIFY)
column15: There are people who keep track of these sorts of things. A_____________of the United Nations is dedicated to tracking global population growth and making estimates of likely future totals. (DIVIDE)
column16: The human population first hit 1 billion in 1800. This means that in mankind's thousands of years of____________on  the planet, our population only reached that figure just over 200 years ago. (EXIST)
column17: In 1927, our population reached 2 billion, and it had become 3 billions by 1960, demonstrating that our numbers are in fact________________increasing, a cause for great concern. (RAPID)
column18: We've been able to survive so far due to____________advances made in agriculture which have increased our food supply. (TECHNOLOGY)
column19: However, unless we improve upon existing techniques, farmers, and the planet itself, will be_____________of supplying enough food for a 10 billion strong global population. And that is only one of many problems that an ever-increasing human population creates. (CAPABLE)
